title: Craspedia minor

This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Craspedia minor include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Craspedia minor is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.

This classification is based on its botanical characteristics, including its morphology and genetic properties.
